text.title: Team Development Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules grounding team-development. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale and is referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: When staging a team from indirect signals the report MUST emit a `tuckman_confidence` score in [0.0, 1.0] AND `tuckman_evidence` (≥ 2 distinct observables: throughput shape, conflict signal, retro theme, 1:1 note). A stage stated without confidence is rejected.

rationale.source: Tuckman 1965; Lencioni — Five Dysfunctions; PMI PMBOK 7 Team Performance Domain

rule.rationale: Tuckman is a model, not a measurement instrument. Confident staging from sparse signals leads to wrong PM actions (e.g. delegating during Storming).

rule.statement: The team charter MUST be co-authored in a ≥ 60-minute workshop with the team. An agent-generated charter is a DRAFT marked `co_authored: false`; it never ships to the team as the final artefact.

rationale.source: Google re:Work — five keys to a successful team; Lencioni team-cohesion research

rule.rationale: Agent-authored charters are generic and lack the buy-in that only participation creates. Working agreements without team input are rules nobody follows.

rule.statement: Every retro action item MUST have a single named owner role AND a sprint-bounded deadline. Items reading "the team will look into this" are rejected and re-prompted.

rationale.source: Atul Gawande — The Checklist Manifesto; Agile retrospective best-practice (Derby/Larsen)

rule.rationale: Actions without owners are not actions; they are intentions that expire at the end of the retro. Deadline-less items live forever in a parking lot.

rule.statement: Synthesised retro outputs, theme files, and `TeamDevelopmentReport` MUST NOT contain individual names. Names present in raw input are stripped before synthesis.

rationale.source: Psychological-safety literature (Edmondson 1999); ICO / GDPR Article 6 data-minimisation guidance

rule.rationale: Individual naming in retro summaries chills honest participation and can cause interpersonal harm. The redact step is part of the methodology, not an HR add-on.

rule.statement: A recurring theme MUST be observed in ≥ 2 distinct sprints before being labelled a pattern. Single-sprint observations are emitted as `single_sprint_observations`, not `patterns`.

rationale.source: PMI PMBOK 7 Lessons-Learned register; Lean retro-pattern research (Derby/Larsen, 2006)

rule.rationale: Single-sprint observations are noise; patterns require recurrence to be actionable. Labelling noise as pattern produces over-fitted interventions.

rule.statement: Skills-gap recommendations MUST evaluate training + pairing options BEFORE proposing a hire. The report carries `gap_options_evaluated: [training, pairing, contracting, hiring]` with explicit cost/time per option.

rationale.source: Buckingham — Now, Discover Your Strengths; PMI knowledge-area cost-management chapter

rule.rationale: Agents default to "hire" because it is the clearest action; training and pairing are faster, cheaper, and improve retention. Skipping the evaluation locks in the most expensive option by default.
